---
title: "Appendix"
format: html
editor: visual
---

## Appendix

We modeled the presence of cetacean eDNA in a given biological replicate at site $s$ and depth $d$, $z_{s,d}$, as a Bernoulli random variable with occurrence probability $\Psi_d$:

$$ z_{s,d} \sim \text{Bernoulli}(\Psi_{d}) $$

where probability of presence in a sample at depth $\Psi_d$ is a smooth function of sample depth $D_d$:

$$ \text{logit}(\Psi_d) = s(d) \cdot D_d. $$

We then modeled the observed detection of cetacean eDNA in each technical replicate $x_t$ as a Bernoulli random variable with primer-specific detection probability $\Omega_\text{primer}$ conditional on presence in the biological sample $z_{s,d}$:

$$ x_t \sim \text{Bernoulli}({\Omega_\text{primer}} \cdot z_{s,d}). $$
